content.title: Spearman ρ=0.315 (p=2.08e-03, 71% sign concordance) confirms Fur derepression as a major component of our signal (n=93 Leaden DEGs)
content.caption: Leaden Δfur logFC vs our 4584-vs-4580 logFC; concordant_strong in dark blue, SspB-buffered set in orange, discordant in red; n=93 Δfur DEGs, Spearman ρ=0.315, p=2.08e-03.

speaker_notes_seed:
This scatter is the central evidence for S1’s driver claim. X-axis: Leaden 2018 Δfur logFC across 93 DEGs (PMID 30210482, supp. Table 2). Y-axis: our 4584-vs-4580 logFC for the same 93 genes. Spearman ρ=0.315, p=2.08e-03, 71% sign concordance: REPORT §Finding 1 frames this as ‘confirming Fur derepression as a major component of our signal’ — not the sole driver, not a full explanation. The concordant-strong cluster (dark blue, top-right) shows amplified derepression relative to Leaden; HutA at +10.5 vs Leaden’s +2.2 is the extreme outlier reflecting the combined dual-release effect. The orange buffered cluster (n=53) near y≈0 spans Leaden’s full −9 to −5 range — ΔsspB is shielding the cbb3/fix respiratory operon at the transcript level.

This slide claims Fur derepression is a major component of our signal. It does NOT yet claim the signal is constitutive rather than iron-status — that discrimination is on the next slide (slide 8), where Leaden’s own iron-limitation comparison and the Zik 2022 genetic anchoring actually appear. For a ‘ρ=0.315 is modest’ challenge: this is one of several converging lines of evidence, deliberately scoped.

revision_log.summary: Removed 'constitutive derepression' from title (claim belongs on slide 8 with the iron-discrimination evidence, not here); replaced with 'Fur derepression as a major component of our signal' (verbatim REPORT §Literature Context, Leaden 2018 entry); added p=2.08e-03 and 71% sign concordance inline so the ρ=0.315 claim is self-hedged; caption and data_source unchanged; speaker notes updated to make the constitutive-vs-iron hand-off to slide 8 explicit.
